violations.text: The metal sheet panels were observed not secured tightly to the wall. Panels can cause create openings/areas for breeding for insects. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2025

The following was observed: 1) The floor tiles/grout lines in the kitchen were observed to be cracked/lifting. Floor tiles/grout lines must be maintained in good repair in order to prevent pooling water. (Per the Owner, establishment is to have tile/grout work completed within next month from inspection date.) 2) The metal sheet panels were observed not secured tightly to the wall. Panels can cause create openings/areas for breeding for insects. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2025

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by one live and one dead cockroach observed in the facility. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies, and removing any dead pests to prevent any accumulation. Current pest control program is set for 1x weekly inspections/treatments. PIC is to increase pest control to 2x weekly until pest issue is resolved. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

The floor tiles/grout lines in the kitchen were observed to be cracked/lifting. Floor tiles/grout lines must be maintained in good repair in order to prevent pooling water. (Per the Owner, establishment is to have tile/grout work completed within next month from inspection date.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2025

The floor tiles/grout lines in the kitchen was observed to be cracked/lifting. Floor tiles/grout lines must be maintained in good repair in order to prevent pooling water. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2025
The hand sink in the warewashing area was not dispensing hot water at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment to use the dishwasher for washing and rinsing ONLY. All equipment and utensils to be sanitized at the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Employee food/beverages were observed stored in the refrigeration units at the cookline. Person in charge notified that employees shall store foods in designated areas only. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device that is in good repair and proper adjustment for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the food prep area and dry storage shelving in the kitchen. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
Squeegee's were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Portobello sauce and other items were observed held at 92-94 degrees Fahrenheit in the steam table. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated items to 165 and heat setting was turned up on the steam table. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
Reduced oxygen packaged salmon labeled "KEEP FROZEN" was observed thawing with the package intact. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
A knife at the magnet holder in the prep area was observed with chips along the blade. Person in charge removed knife from prep area to be serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
The hand sink near the meat display observed to be leaking. The plumbing system in an establishment must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

The floors in the: 1) Bar and the bread/meat display area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris 2) Dry storage/office area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings 3) walk-in freezer observed with over accumulation of ice - The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
